title: Phegopteris oligophlebia
taxonomy: Phegopteris oligophlebia is classified within the plant family Thelypteridaceae and is further classified as a species of the genus Phegopteris. It belongs to the kingdom Plantae and the division Pteridophyta. This species is also known to be a type of fern, specifically a member of the epiphytic ferns. In terms of its higher taxonomic classification, Phegopteris oligophlebia is placed within the class Polypodiopsida. This classification reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary relationships within the fern family.
uses: Phegopteris oligophlebia is known to be used in traditional medicine, particularly in East Asia. The plant is believed to have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. In traditional Chinese medicine, Phegopteris oligophlebia is used to treat various health issues, including fever and respiratory problems. Additionally, the plant's extracts have been studied for their potential in treating skin conditions and wounds. Further research is needed to confirm the effectiveness of Phegopteris oligophlebia as a medicinal treatment.

parts: Phegopteris oligophlebia, also known as the Oligophlebia maidenhair fern, is a species of fern that has been used in traditional medicine for various purposes. The medicinal parts of this plant are its rhizomes and fronds, which are rich in antioxidants and have anti-inflammatory properties. intro: Phegopteris oligophlebia, also known as the fern species, is a type of vascular plant that belongs to the family Dryopteridaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine to treat various health issues, including inflammation, fever, and respiratory problems, due to its potential health benefits such as antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Phegopteris oligophlebia include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
